channel.title: Frontion Global Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇨🇳🇷🇺⚔️ Chinese-trained Russian soldiers return to fight in Ukraine
item.description: European intelligence agencies report that China secretly trained around 200 Russian military personnel late last year, with some now fighting in Ukraine—a significant escalation in Beijing-Moscow military cooperation.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺💥 Russia strikes Ukrainian Danube port as drones target Moscow
item.description: Russia attacked Izmail, Ukraine's largest Danube port, while Ukraine launched drone strikes toward Moscow—escalating cross-border attacks as the war enters a new phase.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇷🇺🇨🇳💰 Russian rouble hits three-year high against yuan before Putin visit
item.description: The Russian rouble strengthened to its highest level against China's yuan since February 2023 ahead of President Putin's visit to Beijing with new business deals on the agenda.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇹🇼🇺🇸🛡️ Taiwan cautiously optimistic on US arms sales
item.description: Taiwan's defence minister expressed cautious optimism about US arms sales, after Trump said he was still considering whether to proceed with new weapons sales to the Chinese-claimed island.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇦🚁💥 Ukrainian mid-range drone strikes deal double blow to Russia
item.description: Ukraine's growing medium-range drone capabilities are burning oil refineries and disrupting Russia's ground offensive—a significant tactical advantage as both sides escalate strikes.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🦠🇨🇩 Ebola deaths in eastern Congo rise to 131 as outbreak spreads
item.description: Twenty-six more suspected Ebola deaths were recorded in 24 hours in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, bringing the death toll to 131, as the WHO chief expressed deep concern about the outbreak's speed and scale.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️ Russian missile and drone strikes kill four in Ukraine's northern regions
item.description: Russian ballistic missile and drone strikes killed four people and injured several others in Ukraine's northern Chernihiv and Sumy regions, according to Ukrainian officials, as attacks continued on multiple fronts.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 📈🛢️ Global stocks and bonds steady as oil prices ease on Trump Iran comments
item.description: Global markets stabilized after President Trump paused a planned attack on Iran and said there was a good chance of reaching a nuclear deal, sending oil prices lower and calming investor concerns about escalating Middle East tensions.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🏦🌍 G7 finance ministers explore responses to Iran war economic fallout
item.description: France's finance minister urged the IMF and World Bank to help countries most vulnerable to Middle East conflict fallout, as G7 finance ministers met in Paris to address rising energy prices and sanctions policy amid the US-Iran standoff.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇷🇺🇨🇳 Putin says Russia and China ready to back each other on sovereignty issues
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin stated ahead of his visit to Beijing that Russia and China are prepared to support each other on a wide range of issues including national unity and protection of sovereignty, in a video address released before his Tuesday summit with Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey Arrests 110 People in Nationwide ISIS Crackdown
item.description: Turkish counter-terror police detained 110 suspects across the country in simultaneous operations targeting ISIS networks and financing.
item.dc:creator: Al Arabiya

item.title: 🇪🇬 Gunman Kills Eight at Egyptian Transport Hub in Southern Province
item.description: A lone gunman killed eight people and wounded five others at a transport hub in Assiut province before being shot dead by security forces.
item.dc:creator: Al Arabiya

item.title: 🇨🇩 DR Congo Ebola Death Toll Surges to 131 as WHO Raises Alarm
item.description: The estimated death toll from the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has risen sharply to 131, with WHO expressing deep concern over scale and speed.
item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany Begins Privatization of Bailed-Out Energy Giant Uniper
item.description: The German government announced the sale of its stake in Uniper, the energy company nationalized during the 2022 gas crisis, marking a major step in post-crisis restructuring.
item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: 🇪🇸 Former Spanish PM Zapatero Investigated for Influence Peddling
item.description: A Spanish court is investigating former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero for alleged influence peddling and related crimes in connection with an airline bailout during his tenure, adding to political turmoil in Spain.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇰🇷🇯🇵 South Korea and Japan Expand Energy Cooperation Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: South Korea and Japan agreed to expand cooperation on LNG and crude oil supply, including stockpiling and petroleum product swap arrangements, strengthening security ties as both nations face geopolitical challenges in the region.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: ☢️ Russia Launches Major Nuclear Drills Amid Ukraine War
item.description: Russia began three days of nuclear weapons exercises involving ballistic and cruise missile launches, raising concerns as Belarus simultaneously conducts its own nuclear drills near NATO borders.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Proposes Peace Deal With Reparations and US Troop Withdrawal
item.description: Tehran presented a revised peace proposal to the US via Pakistan, demanding reparations for war damage and withdrawal of American forces from the region, as diplomatic efforts continue amid ongoing conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: ⚡ UK Energy Bills Set to Jump 13% as Iran War Drives Gas Prices
item.description: Britain's household energy price cap is expected to surge 13% this summer, the largest increase since 2023, as the conflict in Iran pushes up wholesale gas and electricity costs.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg

item.title: 🇵🇱 Polish PM Urges Transatlantic Unity After US Troop Deployment Cancelled
item.description: Donald Tusk stressed the importance of Poland's ties to Washington after the Pentagon cancelled plans to deploy 4,000 US-based troops to Poland, calling for NATO unity during a difficult period.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Announces New Authority to "Manage" Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran formed a new body to oversee the Strait of Hormuz, managing ship transits and collecting passage fees. The move comes as Tehran continues its blockade while facing a US maritime embargo on Iranian ports.
item.dc:creator: Al Arabiya

item.title: 🇲🇾 Malaysia PM Anwar Eyes Snap Election Before Fuel Subsidy Crisis Bites
item.description: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim is considering a snap election before fuel subsidy costs—potentially 4 billion ringgit monthly due to the Iran war—force spending cuts that would hurt his chances. His coalition is fraying with partners defecting.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: ☢️ Belarus Holds Nuclear Weapons Drills with Russia
item.description: Belarus began training exercises involving Russian nuclear weapons, drawing strong condemnation from Ukraine amid escalating regional tensions.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇨🇺 US Sanctions Cuban Officials and Intelligence Agency
item.description: The Trump administration imposed sanctions on 11 Cuban officials including the communications minister and military leaders, escalating pressure on the communist government.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Says China Greatest Source of Regional Instability
item.description: Taiwan condemned China as the primary source of regional unease as Beijing sent a carrier task force into the Western Pacific for training exercises.
item.dc:creator: Reuters
